text.title: files-reference Core Rules
text.summary: Five rules for using the catalog as a routing index — never as final source of truth.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one topical section (not the whole catalog) before scanning slugs.
rule.rationale: Loading the full catalog wastes context budget and overwhelms the picker with false positives. One section is ~10-20 slugs, manageable.

rule.statement: Return at most 2 candidate slugs per task. Each slug must come verbatim from a section of this catalog.
rule.rationale: More than two candidates dilutes the downstream load step; verbatim slugs prevent hallucinated paths.

rule.statement: Resolve every candidate slug with Glob `skills/faion/knowledge/**/<slug>/AGENTS.md` before declaring it valid.
rule.rationale: The catalog can drift behind the directory tree. Glob is cheap and catches stale entries before downstream load.

rule.statement: If a candidate fails Glob resolution, report it as "not found" and escalate. Never invent or guess a corrected path.
rule.rationale: Fabricated paths get loaded as 404s downstream and the agent then hallucinates content. Escalation triggers a catalog refresh task.

rule.statement: Output the final report as JSON candidates only — never paste raw catalog rows into the downstream sub-agent prompt.
rule.rationale: Pasting raw rows multiplies prompt size and re-routes the sub-agent through stale list content. Resolved absolute paths are the only contract.

rule.statement: All preconditions hold: task maps to one section, ≤2 candidates picked, every candidate Glob-verified.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's positive branch.

rule.statement: Task does not map to a named section, or Glob access is unavailable — defer to semantic Grep instead.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's negative branch.
